name: user.create_third_person_ground_character
title: Create Third Person Ground Character
description: Project-local Python user tool that dry-runs or places a controllable ground character in the current level for PIE, preferring real third-person template characters and explicitly reporting non-third-person fallbacks.
wouldWrite: Spawn or update the labeled character actor in the current editor level.
Set the placed actor auto_possess_player value.
Clear an existing bare /Script/Engine.GameModeBase world override if present.
Best-effort configure CharacterMovement, SpringArm, and Camera components.
Mark the actor/map dirty and save dirty map packages.

dryRun.description: If true, return wouldWrite preview without mutation.

location.description: World location for the placed or updated character.

label.description: Actor label to create or update.

classPath.description: Optional explicit Character-derived class path. User-supplied classes are reported as unverified fallbacks unless they are known third-person template classes.

characterClassPolicy.description: Class selection policy. auto tries known third-person classes, then a basic Character fallback; requireThirdPerson disables the basic fallback.

autoPossessPlayer.description: If true, set the placed actor to Auto Possess Player 0 for PIE.

reason: User-generated Python tool; not part of canonical core registry.
notes: Scaffolded by unreal.scaffold_mcp_tool implementationTrack=python. sha256 must match main.py after edits (re-run mcp_user_registry_reload with acceptChangedHashes=true). BP_TwinStickCharacter is not treated as a third-person success.
